title: rosa dumalis subsp. boissieri
intro: Rosa dumalis subsp. boissieri, also known as Boissier's wild rose or Rosa boissieri, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa dumalis subsp. boissieri include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
